welcome.previewText: Welcome to AI PEER! This is a preview of your settings.
welcome.lead: Welcome to AI PEER. We'll help you personalize your experience in just a few simple steps.
welcome.chooseTextSizeSub: Select a comfortable reading size that works best for you
welcome.previewNote: *Preview adjusts as you change text size.

welcome.soundAlertsSubtitle: Enable or disable alert sounds for notifications

questionnaire.question3: Getting in or out of a chair
questionnaire.question5: Reaching up for something above your head or on the ground
questionnaire.question6: Walking up or down a slope
questionnaire.question7: Going out to a social event (e.g. religious service, family gathering, or club meeting)
questionnaire.notAnsweredError: Please select an answer before continuing
questionnaire.lowDescription: You have expressed low concern about falling during daily activities. Continue with your current activities and maintain your strength and balance.
lowRecs: Schedule annual check-ups with your doctor
questionnaire.moderateDescription: You have expressed moderate concern about falling. Consider discussing these concerns with your healthcare provider and implementing fall prevention strategies.
moderateRecs: Talk to your doctor about fall prevention
Consider a balance and strength training program
Review your medications with your healthcare provider
questionnaire.highDescription: You have expressed high concern about falling during daily activities. We strongly recommend discussing these concerns with your healthcare provider as soon as possible.
highRecs: Schedule an appointment with your doctor promptly
Ask about a referral to a physical therapist
Have a thorough home safety evaluation
Consider using assistive devices if recommended
Review all medications with your healthcare provider
questionnaire.subtitle: Fall Risk Questionnaire
questionnaire.info2: Rate how concerned you would be about falling during each activity
questionnaire.info3: If you do not do an activity, answer how concerned you would be if you did
questionnaire.howConcerned: How concerned are you that you might fall:
questionnaire.thankYou: Thank you for completing the assessment!
questionnaire.unableToSaveResults: Unable to save results. Please try again.

exercise.warmupPurpose: Light activities to prepare for exercise
exercise.balanceSubtitle: Exercises to enhance balance and prevent falls

exercise-session.enableCamera: Tap Start Monitoring to enable camera
exercise-session.placePhone: Please place your phone so your full body is visible
exercise-session.tip1: Place your phone so your full body is visible

video-confirm.pageSub: This confirmation step ensures the Vision model knows exactly which exercise you are performing.

balance-test.subtitle: Balance Test
balance-test.centerSub: Two clinical tests to assess your balance and mobility
balance-test.test1Desc: Stand up and sit down as many times as possible in 30 seconds, with your arms crossed over your chest.
balance-test.test1Purpose: Measures lower-body strength and endurance — a key fall-risk indicator.
balance-test.test2Desc: Stand up from a chair, walk to a marker, turn around, walk back, and sit down — as quickly as you safely can.
balance-test.test2Purpose: Measures functional mobility — completing in 12 seconds or less is considered normal.

chair-rise-test.cameraPromptStart: Tap Start Test to enable the camera.
chair-rise-test.defaultCameraPrompt: Sit in a chair facing the camera with your arms crossed across your chest.
chair-rise-test.footnote: Compared to a generic elderly threshold. Age- and gender-specific norms can be more accurate.
chair-rise-test.tip1: Sit upright in a sturdy chair without armrests
chair-rise-test.tip2: Cross your arms across your chest
chair-rise-test.tip3: Stand up fully and sit back down — repeat
chair-rise-test.tip4: As many full reps as you can in 30 seconds
chair-rise-test.tip5: The phone announces each rep aloud
chair-rise-test.ttsStart: Cross your arms. Stand up and sit down as many times as you can. Go.

tug-test.cameraPromptStart: Tap Start Test to enable the camera.
tug-test.defaultCameraPrompt: Sit in a chair facing the camera with 8-10 feet of clear floor space ahead.
tug-test.cdcFootnote: CDC threshold: under 12 seconds is considered normal; 12 seconds or more indicates increased fall risk.
tug-test.tip1: Sit in a sturdy chair facing the camera
tug-test.tip2: Place a marker about 3 meters (10 feet) away
tug-test.tip4: Walk to the marker, turn around, walk back
tug-test.tip5: Sit down — the test ends automatically
tug-test.tip6: Raise both arms overhead to start when ready
tug-test.stateWaitingForStand: Stand up to start the test
tug-test.stateWalking: Walking — go to the marker and back
tug-test.ttsStart: Stand up, walk to the marker, turn around, and come back to sit.

gesture-overlay.promptTitle: Hold up an open palm to start
gesture-overlay.promptSub: Show your palm to the camera for half a second
gesture-overlay.ttsPrompt: Hold up an open palm to start.

FRAMatrixCard.enterBtrackScore: Enter your new BTrackS score in cm:
FRAMatrixCard.riskBasedOn: Based on alignment of physical fall risk (BTrackS) and perceived fall risk (FES-I).
